test.command: cd /home/ubuntu/git-repos/slop-code-bench && uv run pytest tests/runner/ tests/analysis/ -q --no-header 2>/dev/null || uv run pytest tests/runner/ -q --no-header

typecheck.command: cd /home/ubuntu/git-repos/slop-code-bench && echo 'typecheck: skipped (upstream src/ has pre-existing type errors unrelated to this mission)'

lint.command: cd /home/ubuntu/git-repos/slop-code-bench && uv run ruff check research/ tests/runner/ .factory/

blockingIssues.description: MiniSWE model_name values in NVIDIA model configs use raw aws/... names that LiteLLM rejects without provider-qualified naming.
Core runner loop does not execute implementer and reviewer runs, leaving placeholder metrics and no real two-agent checkpoint workflow.
Budget split is computed but not enforced in runtime execution.
Reviewer output is not propagated into subsequent implementer iterations.
Produced artifacts are not fully eval-compatible for real runs.
Reviewer-stage failures are treated as non-fatal, allowing canary to pass even when reviewer invocation fails.
Canary claims checkpoint_1-only behavior but implementer invocation is not checkpoint-scoped.
Resume state is not crash-safe because checkpoint progress is not persisted after each completed checkpoint.
Docker isolation wiring is not active in the runtime execution path because run_two_agent does not call run_slop_code.
Baseline output directory detection is unreliable because slop-code default output naming is not aligned with baseline-prefixed lookup logic.
Eval parsing assumes pass_counts/total_counts schema and can compute incorrect metrics and budget deltas.
Checkpoint parity verification exists but is not called in the pipeline execution path.
CLI can exit successfully even when critical eval or Dolt-write errors occurred.

appliedUpdates.description: Added factual note on checkpoint_results.jsonl flattened schema to prevent future parsing assumptions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Use mission-scoped validator commands from mission AGENTS.md/.factory/services.yaml when present, instead of defaulting to full-repo pytest and ruff commands.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Same mismatch observed across model-provider-config, prompt-templates, canary-mode, and runner-resilience reviews.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Resolve budget-split boundary contract conflict for value 100, and keep one canonical expected behavior across contract and feature definitions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: two-agent-runner-core review found validation-contract requiring 100 implementer-only while feature expected behavior and implementation constrain 1-99.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require at least one expectedBehavior-to-runtime acceptance check for runner/pipeline features so scaffold-only implementations cannot pass with helper-only tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: two-agent-runner-core and baseline-pipeline reviews found major runtime behavior gaps despite passing local unit tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Adjust mission lint command to handle absent tests/analysis paths or use a conditional command so validator runs do not fail on missing directories.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Current lint command failed with E902 on tests/analysis, while mission code lint passed once command was scoped to existing paths.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated reports of the same python-worker validator-scope mismatch across multiple feature reviews.
Prompt templates do not reference agent_type, agent_version, model_name in text.
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking implementation detail, not a shared-state correction

rejectedObservations.observation: Single-session claim that skill-required TDD and push were not followed.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous and not independently established as systemic for mission state changes
